commit.message:
fix(model-3d): merge coplanar wall faces to prevent stretch artifacts

After Case 3 extrude, gap-filling triangle walls share vertices with
moved corners. Subsequent extrudes move those corners, stretching the
triangles into large sloping faces. Fix: merge coplanar wall faces
into adjacent side faces immediately after creation. The side face
absorbs the triangle into a larger polygon that stretches correctly.

commit.message:
Add adaptive subdivision when geosearch hits 500-result limit

When a Wikipedia geosearch query returns the maximum 500 results,
the area is automatically split into 4 quadrants at half the radius
and re-queried. Recurses until queries no longer overflow or the
radius drops below 200m. Eliminates data loss in dense urban areas.

commit.message:
Fix adaptive tolerance test, sync with paper v45, fix PyTorch shm.dll

- Fix test_adaptive_tolerance: tolerance is monotonically increasing with
  prune fraction (not tighter at low f — the formula doesn't work that way).
  Now checks monotonicity + detection rate >= 90%. Exp 10: 7/7 PASS.

- Exp 14: 11/11 PASS (verified on RTX 3090). All adversarial and cross-seed
  stability tests confirmed.

- Paper v45: Exp 14 expanded to 11 tests, Exp 15 with 5-test breakdown,
  I-14 aligned with shared/config.py implementation, 15 experiments ~80 tests.

- PyTorch fix: shm.dll failure caused by missing CUPTI DLL directory in
  torch __init__.py. Added extras/CUPTI/lib64 to DLL search path.
  Torch 2.9.1a0+gitd38164a now loads successfully with CUDA 12.9.

Verified: Experiments 01-07 all PASS, Exp 10 all PASS, Exp 14 all PASS.
Experiments 08, 12, 13, 15 running (MINE training in progress).

commit.message:
fix: remove invalid 'local' outside function in deployment config write

SC2168: 'local' is only valid inside function bodies. The atomic write
of .deployment_config uses a temp variable at the top-level script scope,
so the 'local' declaration was illegal and caused ShellCheck CI to fail.

commit.message:
feat: add USER.md support for user profile context (#497)

Follow OpenClaw's SOUL.md/USER.md pattern: contractors can now write
free-form notes about themselves that the assistant uses to personalize
responses. USER.md is stored alongside SOUL.md in the contractor's
data directory and injected into both the agent and heartbeat system
prompts as an "About Your User" / "About the user" section.

Backend: load/save USER.md in file_store, expose user_text in profile
API, include in system prompt builder.
Frontend: add "About You / USER" textarea to the Assistant settings tab.

Fixes #496

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reorganize project structure and add comprehensive test suite

## Changes

**Project Structure**:
- Moved core modules (analyzer, preprocessor, server) into log_context_mcp/ package
- Created tests/ directory with comprehensive test suite (56 tests)
- Added proper package __init__.py files and updated all imports to be fully qualified

**Build Configuration**:
- Updated pyproject.toml to use packages instead of py-modules
- Added pytest configuration (testpaths, python_files)
- Added optional dev dependencies for testing (pytest, pytest-asyncio, pytest-cov)
- Updated entry point to log_context_mcp.server:mcp.run

**Testing**:
- 56 comprehensive tests covering all three layers
  - Layer 1 (deterministic): 35 tests for ANSI stripping, severity detection, deduplication, timestamps, noise removal, stack traces
  - Prompt generation: 4 tests for Layer 2 prompt structure
  - Backend resolution: 13 tests for auto-detection and explicit backend selection
  - Backend implementations: 4 tests for API request formatting (mocked)
- All tests run without external APIs (~72% coverage)
- Pytest configuration for easy test discovery

**Documentation**:
- Enhanced README.md with new project structure, venv setup instructions, and test documentation
- Created .claude.md with detailed project guide for future Claude sessions
- Documented backend-agnostic LLM support (Anthropic native + OpenAI-compatible)

## Testing
All 56 tests pass. Run with:
  python3.11 -m pytest tests/ -v
  python3.11 -m pytest tests/ --cov=log_context_mcp --cov-report=term-missing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: log-context-mcp
repository.description: An MCP server that sits between raw logs and the LLM context window. Instead of dumping thousands of log lines into the context (burning tokens on noise), the coding agent calls `log_ingest` and gets back a structured, deduplicated summary in ~500-2000 tokens. It can then drill down into specific patterns on demand.

fix(audience): deduplicate follower entries by contact (JOV-1360) (#5323)

Use DISTINCT ON (COALESCE(phone, email)) with a subquery to keep only
the most recent subscription per unique contact identifier. Fixes the
issue where the same phone number appeared twice in the followers list
(35d and 40d ago entries) because the unique indexes allow a row with
phone+null-email and a row with the same phone+non-null-email to coexist.

Applies to both the server-side audience-data.ts (SSR path) and the
/api/dashboard/audience/subscribers route (client-side API path).

Fix Kalshi initialized market poisoning signal engine with fake 0¢ prices

When no active Kalshi market exists (between settlement windows), the
fallback to initialized markets fed yesPrice=0 into scoring, causing
false "EXTREME NO" display, -18% confidence penalty, and crowd veto
triggering on valid trades. Now only active market prices feed into
G.kalshiYesPrice; UI shows dashes and "MARKET OPENING" for inactive.

commit.message:
fix: IPv6 NAT rewrite skipped reverse SNAT/DNAT destination rewrite

nat_rewrite_v6 gated IP/port rewrites on nat_flags (SESS_FLAG_SNAT →
rewrite src, SESS_FLAG_DNAT → rewrite dst). For reverse SNAT, the
pre-routing dnat_table correctly rewrites meta->dst_ip to the original
client, but conntrack overwrites meta->nat_flags with only SESS_FLAG_SNAT
from the session — losing the SESS_FLAG_DNAT that pre-routing set.
Result: nat_rewrite_v6 skips the destination rewrite, reply goes out
with wrong destination, 100% packet loss on IPv6 transit traffic.

Additionally, zone_ct_update_v6 (the fast-path for established sessions)
dispatched to xdp_nat only when it modified meta fields (nat_needed flag),
but reverse SNAT doesn't modify meta in conntrack — the pre-routing
dnat_table already did the work. So the fast-path skipped NAT entirely.

The fix has two parts:

1. nat_rewrite_v6: Remove flag gating, unconditionally compare meta vs
   packet fields (matching nat_rewrite_v4 behavior). Any field that
   differs gets rewritten regardless of which NAT flags are set.

2. zone_ct_update_v6 + handle_ct_hit_v6: Dispatch to xdp_nat based on
   session NAT flags (sess->flags & SNAT|DNAT) rather than whether
   conntrack modified meta fields. This matches zone_ct_update_v4 and
   handle_ct_hit_v4 which already use this pattern.

Tested: IPv6 ping through loss and local HA clusters — 0% packet loss.
IPv4 unaffected (nat_rewrite_v4 already uses unconditional comparison).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Widen content area to 85vw, add webhook logging, fix sync-products parse error (#14)

  - logs/*.log excluded via .gitignore; logs/.gitkeep tracks the directory
- Fix PHP parse error in scripts/sync-products.php line 302 (and 321):
  unescaped ASCII " inside double-quoted strings terminated strings early;
  replaced with \" so variable interpolation works correctly

commit.message:
Add agent web UI (FastAPI + HTMX + Jinja2)

oly-agent/web/:
- app.py: FastAPI app with Jinja2 templates, custom filters (fmt_weight,
  fmt_rpe, fmt_pct, reps_list, status_color, phase_color)
- deps.py: shared get_db dependency + ATHLETE_ID constant
- jobs.py: ThreadPoolExecutor background queue for program generation
- routers/: dashboard, program, log_session, generate
- queries/: dashboard (current week, adherence, warnings),
  program (full week tree, activate), log_session (create log + exercises)
- templates/: base, dashboard, program_list, program, log_session, generate
- partials/: generate_result (HTMX polling), exercise_log_section,
  exercise_logged_row, status_badge

Key interactions:
- Dashboard shows current week sessions with logged/unlogged status
- Program view: week accordions with full exercise tables, activate button
- Log session: two-phase form (session header → exercise logging)
  with click-to-prefill from prescribed exercises
- Generate: triggers orchestrator in background thread, polls every 3s via HTMX

Run: cd oly-agent && PYTHONUTF8=1 uv run uvicorn web.app:app --reload --port 8080
